figures.caption: Figure 1. (a) Occlusions and ambiguous boundaries in 2D predictions lead to inaccurate 3D segmentation, whereas our geometryguided modeling effectively rectifies these errors. (b) The baseline suffers from the lack of geometric relation modeling, whereas our method exploits inter-instance geometric constraints to produce accurate predictions.

Table 1. Open-vocabulary 3D semantic segmentation results. Comparison between GeoGuide and existing fully-supervised and zero-shot methods. SAS∗ denotes using the same 2D features as in SAS [42], ‡ denotes the use of the 2D-3D ensemble strategy, LS: LSeg, OS: OpenSeg. Best results are shown in bold.

Table 3. Cross-domain generalization evaluation. Models are trained on ScanNet v2 and evaluated on Matterport3D (21/40/80/160 categories), † denotes results reproduced by us using officially provided weights, ‡ denotes the use of the 2D-3D ensemble strategy. SAS∗ denotes using the same 2D features as in SAS [42].
Figure 2. Overview of our proposed GeoGuide framework. GeoGuide integrates hierarchical geometric priors to achieve geometry–semantic consistent open-vocabulary 3D segmentation
Figure 3. Preliminary Experiments. Comparison between OpenScene and our preliminary attempt under different 2D models. LS: LSeg, OS: OpenSeg.
